beats.narration: Every radiograph is made twice: exposure builds the invisible latent image, the darkroom makes it visible, and one light leak ruins it.

payload.title: THE DARKROOM
payload.sub: Where the latent image becomes a radiograph.

beats.narration: The facility comes first: a light tight room, light trapped vents, and a strict split. Film on the dry side, chemistry on the wet.
Manual tanks are cheap but every variable is yours. The automatic processor locks time and temperature: dry film in eight to twelve minutes.

payload.heading: Manual tanks vs. automatic processor
payload.note: TIME + TEMP LOCKED · DRY FILM IN 8–12 MIN

beats.narration: A direct safelight: fifteen watts behind a filter matched to your film, four feet or more from the bench. The coin test catches fog.

payload.heading: The classic safelight rule
payload.note: SEE A COIN OUTLINE? THE BENCH ISN'T SAFE

beats.narration: The darkroom's golden rule: a safelight is slow, not safe. Handle film fast, keep bins closed, prove your lights with coins.

payload.sub: Fast hands, closed bins, coins on film.
